provenance.settings: scf.RHF(mol).density_fit(auxbasis=...); conv_tol 1e-11; cart=False (spherical); orbital AND auxiliary bases built from vendored data/basis/*.json (BSE), never PySCF's internal library, so hartree and the oracle use byte-identical exponents/coefficients and the identical fit space.
provenance.tolerances: Same fit, same integrals: hartree<->ref gate 1e-9 Eh. (The shared ~1e-4 Eh fitting error vs non-fitted HF cancels.)
provenance.note: water/6-31g was deliberately replaced by water/def2-svp: hartree's 6-31G is Cartesian while the JKFIT aux set is spherical, and PySCF's DF applies mol.cart to the auxiliary basis too, so it cannot mirror hartree's cart-orbital/spherical-aux combination.
